description: Practice building and extending a CSS design system

challenges.title: Build a Toggle Component with Design Tokens
challenges.description: Build a CSS toggle/switch component using your design system tokens. Requirements: (1) uses only CSS (no JS for styling), (2) uses design system tokens for all colors and spacing (--color-primary, --color-surface, etc.), (3) has on/off visual states, (4) smooth transition animation, (5) includes :focus-visible styles, (6) works with <label> for accessibility.
hints: Hide the actual checkbox input with opacity: 0 or position: absolute
Use ::before on a label or span to create the toggle track
Use ::after for the circular knob that slides
Use the :checked pseudo-class on the hidden input for the 'on' state
Transition the transform of the knob for smooth sliding
challenges.solution: .c-toggle { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--spacing-sm); cursor: pointer; } .c-toggle__input { position: absolute; opacity: 0; width: 0; height: 0; } .c-toggle__track { position: relative; width: 44px; height: 24px; background: var(--color-border); border-radius: var(--radius-full); transition: background var(--transition-fast); } .c-toggle__knob { position: absolute; top: 2px; left: 2px; width: 20px; height: 20px; background: white; border-radius: 50%; transition: transform var(--transition-fast); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); } .c-toggle__input:checked + .c-toggle__track { background: var(--color-primary); } .c-toggle__input:checked + .c-toggle__track .c-toggle__knob { transform: translateX(20px); } .c-toggle__input:focus-visible + .c-toggle__track { outline: 2px solid var(--color-primary); outline-offset: 2px; } .c-toggle__label { font-family: var(--font-sans); color: var(--color-text); }

challenges.title: Implement Multi-Theme Token System
challenges.description: Extend a basic design system with a multi-theme token system. Requirements: (1) define at least 20 design tokens in :root (colors, spacing, typography, shadows), (2) create 3 complete themes: light (default), dark, and high-contrast, (3) themes are activated via data-attributes (data-theme="dark", data-theme="high-contrast"), (4) include a page with a Button, Card, and Badge component using the tokens, (5) add a JavaScript theme switcher, (6) persist the theme choice in localStorage.
hints: Define theme overrides with [data-theme="dark"] selector over only the tokens that change
Components should all reference var(--color-*) = no hard-coded values
High-contrast theme should maximize contrast (black/white, thick borders)
For localStorage: store 'dark' or 'light' string, read on page load

challenges.title: Design System Component Audit & Documentation
challenges.description: Audit an existing (provided) CSS codebase and create a design system from it. Requirements: (1) extract all hard-coded values into CSS custom properties (design tokens), (2) organize into ITCSS layers with @layer, (3) create BEM naming for all components, (4) create documentation for each component (variants, states, accessibility), (5) build a living style guide page showing all components with code snippets, (6) add dark theme support by overriding token values, (7) test all components and fix any accessibility issues found (focus styles, contrast, semantic HTML).
hints: Use grep/stylelint to find hard-coded colors, font sizes, and spacing values
Each component needs: base styles + variant modifiers + state styles
Documentation should show: component name, HTML example, variants, accessibility notes
The living style guide should be a single HTML page with all components
Check contrast ratios with browser DevTools
